initial_program:
% Grid dimensions.
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{ R : input(R, _, _, _) }.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{ C : input(_, C, _, _) }.

% Choose an offset (must be a nonnegative column index smaller than both dimensions).
1 { off(Off) : true } 1 :- off_cond(Off).
off_cond(Off) :- Off >= 0, Off < N, Off < M.

% For cells that lie to the right of the offset column: shift down and left.
output(Rp,Cp,Color) :- input(R,C,Color),
                         C >= off,
                         Rp = R + off,
                         Cp = C - off,
                         Rp >= 0, Rp < N,
                         Cp >= 0, Cp < M,
                         off(off).

% For cells that lie to the left of the offset column: shift down and right.
output(Rp,Cp,Color) :- input(R,C,Color),
                         C < off,
                         Rp = R + off,
                         Cp = C + off,
                         Rp >= 0, Rp < N,
                         Cp >= 0, Cp < M,
                         off(off).

% A cell receives a colour iff it appears in either of the two output rules above.
target(R,C) :- output(R,C,_).

% Exactly one colour per output cell.
1 { output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- true.

% No cell may have two different colours.
:- output(R,C,Color1), output(R,C,Color2), Color1 != Color2.

% Cells without a target must be zero.
:- output(R,C,Color), Color != 0, not target(R,C), output(R,C,0).

#show output/3.

clingo_check errors:
<block>:6:1-40: error: unsafe variables in:
  off(Off):-[#inc_base];Off<M;Off<N;Off>=0.
<block>:6:38-39: note: 'M' is unsafe
<block>:6:29-30: note: 'N' is unsafe
<block>:6:5-8: note: 'Off' is unsafe

rounds.program_after:
% Grid dimensions.
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{ R : input(R, _, _, _) }.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{ C : input(_, C, _, _) }.

% Choose an offset (must be a nonnegative column index smaller than both dimensions).
off(Off) :- Off >= 0, Off < N, Off < M.
1 { off(Off) : true } 1 :- true.

% For cells that lie to the right of the offset column: shift down and left.
output(Rp,Cp,Color) :- input(R,C,Color),
                         C >= off,
                         Rp = R + off,
                         Cp = C - off,
                         Rp >= 0, Rp < N,
                         Cp >= 0, Cp < M,
                         off(off).

% For cells that lie to the left of the offset column: shift down and right.
output(Rp,Cp,Color) :- input(R,C,Color),
                         C < off,
                         Rp = R + off,
                         Cp = C + off,
                         Rp >= 0, Rp < N,
                         Cp >= 0, Cp < M,
                         off(off).

% A cell receives a colour iff it appears in either of the two output rules above.
target(R,C) :- output(R,C,_).

% Exactly one colour per output cell.
% Exactly one colour per output cell (handled elsewhere).

% No cell may have two different colours.
:- output(R,C,Color1), output(R,C,Color2), Color1 != Color2.

% Cells without a target must be zero.
:- output(R,C,Color), Color != 0, not target(R,C), output(R,C,0).

#show output/3.

rounds.program_after:
% Grid dimensions.
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{ R : input(R, _, _, _) }.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{ C : input(_, C, _, _) }.

% Choose an offset (must be a nonnegative column index smaller than both dimensions).
off(Off) :- Off >= 0, Off < N, Off < M.
1 { off(Off) : true } 1 :- true.

% For cells that lie to the right of the offset column: shift down and left.
output(Rp,Cp,Color) :- input(R,C,Color),
                         C >= off,
                         Rp = R + off,
                         Cp = C - off,
                         Rp >= 0, Rp < N,
                         Cp >= 0, Cp < M,
                         off(off).

% For cells that lie to the left of the offset column: shift down and right.
output(Rp,Cp,Color) :- input(R,C,Color),
                         C < off,
                         Rp = R + off,
                         Cp = C + off,
                         Rp >= 0, Rp < N,
                         Cp >= 0, Cp < M,
                         off(off).

% A cell receives a colour iff it appears in either of the two output rules above.
target(R,C) :- output(R,C,_).

% Exactly one colour per output cell.
% Exactly one colour per output cell (handled elsewhere).

% No cell may have two different colours.
:- output(R,C,Color1), output(R,C,Color2), Color1 != Color2.

% Cells without a target must be zero.
:- output(R,C,Color), Color != 0, not target(R,C), output(R,C,0).

#show output/3.

final_program:
% Grid dimensions.
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{ R : input(R, _, _, _) }.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{ C : input(_, C, _, _) }.

% Choose an offset (must be a nonnegative column index smaller than both dimensions).
off(Off) :- Off >= 0, Off < N, Off < M.
1 { off(Off) : true } 1 :- true.

% For cells that lie to the right of the offset column: shift down and left.
output(Rp,Cp,Color) :- input(R,C,Color),
                         C >= off,
                         Rp = R + off,
                         Cp = C - off,
                         Rp >= 0, Rp < N,
                         Cp >= 0, Cp < M,
                         off(off).

% For cells that lie to the left of the offset column: shift down and right.
output(Rp,Cp,Color) :- input(R,C,Color),
                         C < off,
                         Rp = R + off,
                         Cp = C + off,
                         Rp >= 0, Rp < N,
                         Cp >= 0, Cp < M,
                         off(off).

% A cell receives a colour iff it appears in either of the two output rules above.
target(R,C) :- output(R,C,_).

% Exactly one colour per output cell.
% Exactly one colour per output cell (handled elsewhere).

% No cell may have two different colours.
:- output(R,C,Color1), output(R,C,Color2), Color1 != Color2.

% Cells without a target must be zero.
:- output(R,C,Color), Color != 0, not target(R,C), output(R,C,0).

#show output/3.
